codeReview.summary: The implementation correctly ports report_missing_positions (Python L353-361) and report_duplicate_anchors (Python L431-440) from yj_to_epub_navigation.py. Both functions match the Python logic 1:1. The Go code removed the erroneous KFX_VERBOSE gate from reportMissingPositions (which never existed in the Python), added correct EID.OFFSET formatting with string sorting and truncation, and changed reportDuplicateAnchors signature to accept explicit usedAnchors map matching Python's used_anchors set pattern. The render.go caller correctly builds the usedAnchors map from resolvedAnchorURI (non-empty href = used anchor). 22 tests cover all 5 validation assertions (VAL-B-019 through VAL-B-023).

sharedStateObservations.observation: The Go code switched from fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, ...) to log.Printf(...) for the reporting functions. This is a departure from the older code in the same file that uses fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, ...). The mission AGENTS.md mentions 'Python log.error(...) → Go structured logging' but doesn't specify whether to use log.Printf or fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr). Consistency within a file would be preferable.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: internal/kfx/yj_to_epub_navigation.go L493 uses log.Printf, while L471 (processPageUnit) uses fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, ...). The diff shows the old reportMissingPositions used fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, ...) and was changed to log.Printf.

summary: PASS. The implementation correctly ports both report_missing_positions and report_duplicate_anchors from the Python reference. The logic, data structures, and control flow match 1:1. The caller in render.go correctly constructs the usedAnchors map from resolvedAnchorURI to parallel Python's used_anchors set. Helper functions (truncatePositionList, sortablePositionStrings) correctly implement Python's truncate_list and string-based sorting. All 22 tests pass with comprehensive coverage of both functions, their edge cases, and related helpers. No bugs or logic deviations found.
